title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers project metadata, assets, pyinstaller 6.21, platforms, and clean launch into a changed portable inspected game release candidate. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of portable inspected game release candidate with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.
Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Project Metadata
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 7423 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Project Metadata.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Asset Licenses
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-asset-licenses-1lzmv5" that uses case 9000 to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Asset Licenses.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Asset Licenses into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Asset Licenses, keep Asset Licenses connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-asset-licenses-1lzmv5
# Competency: asteroids-asset-licenses.
# Case 9000: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_asset_licenses_1lzmv5(target_platforms: int = 3, clean_installs: int = 3, declared_assets: int = 12, packaged_assets: int = 12, licenses_recorded: bool = True, offline_launch: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1lzmv5 = "1lzmv5-5vmzl1-9000"
    assert evidence_variant_1lzmv5
    evidence_axes_1lzmv5 = (
        "player1lzmv5",
        "seed9000",
        "input5vmzl1",
        "frame1lzmv59000",
        "state90001lzmv5",
        "playtest5vmzl19000",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1lzmv5)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or clean_installs < 0:
        return False, target_platforms, "package-domain"
    if clean_installs != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_installs, "platform-gap"
    if packaged_assets != declared_assets:
        return False, packaged_assets, "asset-gap"
    if not licenses_recorded or not offline_launch:
        return False, packaged_assets, "release-input"
    assert packaged_assets == declared_assets
    return True, target_platforms, "portable-build"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Asset Licenses
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 2375 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.
